Continuing Legal

التطوير المهني القانوني المستمر

The 11th year of Dubai's premier legal development programme. 4,411 licensed practitioners. 69 accredited courses. Build your expertise, meet your compliance requirements, and advance your career in law.

COMPLIANCE DEADLINE
31 December 2026
23:59 GST
UTC+4
235
Days
00
Hours
00
Mins
Secs
2026 CYCLE ELAPSED 67%
1 Jan 2026 31 Dec 2026
2026 REQUIREMENTS
16 points.
Every year.

Every licensed legal consultant on the Dubai roll must earn 16 CLPD points before 31 December each year. No extensions. No exceptions.

8
Mandatory activities
Face-to-face and e-learning sessions on prescribed legal topics. Minimum 2 sessions must be face-to-face. Ethics is compulsory for all.
8
Accredited activities
Any approved course from the CLPD catalogue. No single accredited activity may exceed 4 points. Up to 50% may be completed online.
LATEST COURSES

Book a session

69 accredited courses across mandatory and elective categories. New courses added throughout 2026.

NEW FOR 2026

What's changed this year

New podcast series — Q2
On-demand audio content on emerging legal topics. Counts toward your accredited activities. Stay informed on the go.
DIFC Days introduced
In-person sessions in the DIFC. Complete all mandatory requirements in a single streamlined day at the Gate Village.
50% online now allowed
Up to 4 of your 8 mandatory points (50%) can now be completed online, for greater flexibility. Ethics remains compulsory.
NEWS & EVENTS

Latest updates

MANDATORY
5 May 2026
AI Governance e-learning now available in Arabic
The mandatory AI Governance course is now available in Arabic, with English available since March. Both versions count for 2 CLPD points.
DIFC
28 Apr 2026
DIFC Day — 28 May · Limited seats remaining
Only 3 seats remain on the 28 May DIFC Arbitration session. Book immediately at training.legal.dubai.gov.ae to secure your place.
FILING
1 Apr 2026
Filing system is now live for 2026 cycle
Practitioners can now submit completed CLPD points online. The filing deadline is 31 December 2026. Late submissions will not be accepted.
Not registered yet?
Every licensed legal consultant in Dubai must register on the CLPD roll and complete 16 points by 31 December 2026.

All courses › Mandatory
MANDATORY ACTIVITIES · 2026

Mandatory courses

REQUIREMENT
8 mandatory points required per cycle. A minimum of 2 sessions must be face-to-face. Up to 4 points (50%) may be completed online.
ETHICS
Ethics is compulsory for all practitioners. One course must be completed every year. Available online in English and Arabic.
CREDITS
All mandatory courses cost 5 credits each (5 credits = 5 × AED 120). Buy credits in your account to book.
All courses › Accredited
ACCREDITED ACTIVITIES · 2026

Accredited electives

REQUIREMENT
8 accredited points required per cycle. No single accredited activity may exceed 4 points. Choose freely from the approved catalogue.
CARRY-OVER RULE
You may carry over a maximum of 2 excess points to count towards accredited points in the subsequent filing period.
All courses › E-learning
E-LEARNING · ONLINE · SELF-PACED

Online learning

HOW IT WORKS
Once booked, access your e-learning via the Docebo LMS at creativewordtraining.docebosaas.com. Progress is auto-saved and you can start and stop anytime.
2026 RULE CHANGE
Up to 50% of mandatory points (4 out of 8) may now be completed online — an increase from previous years. Ethics is available exclusively online.
CREDITS
E-learning courses also cost 5 credits each. There is no discount for online formats — all courses are priced equally.
Access your enrolled courses
Once you have booked an e-learning course, access it through the Kwintessential learning management system. Login with the credentials emailed to you on booking.
Access LMS platform →
FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS

FAQs — CLPD 2026

For further enquiries: Training.Development@legal.dubai.gov.ae
Can't find your answer?
Contact the CLPD Affairs Committee directly and we'll respond within 2 working days.
+971 4 353 3337
BECOME AN ACCREDITED PROVIDER

Accreditation application

SERVICE CODE PDT-PDAL-SRV67
Become an
Accredited Provider
Training companies, universities, and professional bodies. No fees. 15 working days for review. Pass ≥80%: your courses are listed on the CLPD portal.
REQUIRED DOCUMENTS
1. Articles of Association
2. Accreditation Form A1
3. Company Profile
SERVICE CODE PDT-PDAL-SRV68
Accredit a course
Already an accredited provider? Submit a new course for CLPD accreditation. Courses must be submitted prior to being run. Attendance must be filed within 30 days.
REQUIRED DOCUMENTS
1. Course Accreditation Form A2
2. Course overview & objectives
3. Training materials
4. Trainer profile
15
Working days
for LAD review
No fees
Free service
no application charge
≥80%
Pass threshold
to receive accreditation
APPLICATION PROCESS
${[['Submit','Fill all fields and upload required documents online',1],['LAD Review','Department reviews application, credentials and materials',2],['Assessment','Score ≥80%: provider code issued · Below: rejection with feedback',3],['Listed','Courses listed on CLPD portal for practitioners to book',4]].map(([t,d,n])=>`
${n}
${t}
${d}
`).join('')}
Provider Obligations
1. Submit accreditation request prior to the course being run — retrospective applications are not accepted.
2. Submit a full register of attendees (with practitioner IDs) within 30 days of the course being completed.
3. Accreditation must be renewed annually. Providers who fail to submit attendance records may have accreditation suspended.
MY APPLICATIONS
Apply to become an Accredited Training Provider
Submit your application through the LAD PowerApps portal. A response to your application takes 15 working days. There are no fees for accreditation.
Accreditation Application →
SERVICE CODE PDT-PDAL-SRV67
Provider Accreditation Request
Timeframe15 working days
FeesNo fees
BeneficiariesGovernment, Firms, Training Companies, Professional Bodies
REQUIRED DOCUMENTS
1. Articles of Association
2. Accreditation Form (A1)
3. Company Profile
SERVICE CODE PDT-PDAL-SRV68
Course Accreditation Request
Timeframe15 working days
FeesNo fees

REQUIRED DOCUMENTS
1. Course Accreditation Form (A2)
2. Course overview & objectives

APPLICATION PROCESS
1
Submit application
Fill form and upload required documents via the portal
2
LAD review
Department reviews application and all supporting documents
3
Assessment
Pass ≥80%: Accredited Provider Code issued. Below 80%: rejection email
4
Listed on portal
Approved providers added to the CLPD portal catalogue
⚠️
Provider Obligations
1. Submit accreditation request prior to the course being run.
2. Submit a register of attendees within 30 days of the course being completed.
CLPD FILING SYSTEM · 2026

Submit your CLPD record

Every practicing legal consultant registered on the roll must file their completed CLPD record within 30 days of the filing period lapsing. Deadline: 31 January 2027.

YOUR FILING STATUS · 2026
10
PTS EARNED
6
PTS REMAINING
87
DAYS LEFT
3
COURSES BOOKED
Filing System is now live
Submit your completed CLPD points through the official LAD filing portal. Have your practitioner ID and course completion certificates ready.
Access Filing Portal →
What to prepare
✓Practitioner ID and LAD roll number
✓Completion certificates for all courses attended
✓Attendance records for any internal/accredited sessions
✓Pro bono completion certificates from the portal (if applicable)
✓Signed CLPD self-declaration form
Key deadlines
Programme deadline31 Dec 2026
Filing deadline31 Jan 2027
Record retentionMinimum 5 years
Non-complianceRegistration renewal denied
⚠️
If you fail to file your CLPD record, the CLPD Affairs Committee will send a notice. Continued non-compliance results in non-renewal of your registration and fines under Executive Council Resolution No. 22 of 2011.
🇦🇪
المحامون الإماراتيون

Emirati Advocates Courses

The Legal Affairs Department provides Emirati advocates with free access to a comprehensive range of CLPD courses, enhancing the legal profession and fostering growth of Emirati advocates.

How to enrol — three steps
1
Email the CLPD team
Send an email to clpdadmin@creativeword.ae including a scanned copy of your practicing card and a request to be added to the Emirati Advocates programme.
2
Receive login credentials
The team will email your login details for the Kwintessential learning platform within 2-3 working days.
3
Access the full course library
Log in to access the complete library of CLPD courses — all free for Emirati advocates.
✉ Email to enrol Access platform (existing users)
Already have login credentials? Access the Kwintessential LMS directly. If you've forgotten your password, email clpdadmin@creativeword.ae.
ACCREDITED CLPD ACTIVITY

Voluntary Legal Services
Pro Bono

Earn accredited CLPD points by volunteering your legal expertise through the LAD Voluntary Legal Services Smart Portal. Connect with members of the public seeking legal assistance.

Access the Pro Bono Portal →
How it works
The Voluntary Legal Services Smart Portal connects members of the public seeking legal assistance with volunteering advocates and legal consultants across the Emirate of Dubai. Register and take appointments directly through the portal.
Points earned
4
COMPLETED APPOINTMENTS = 1 POINT
Four completed appointments through the portal = 1 Accredited CLPD Point. Maximum of 4 accredited points per filing period via pro bono.
Legal basis
Administrative Resolution No. (29) of 2024 — Adopting Voluntary Legal Services as an Accredited Continuing Legal Professional Development Activity, issued by the Legal Affairs Department, Government of Dubai.
For more information: probono@legal.dubai.gov.ae · +971 4 353 3337
Access Voluntary Legal Services Portal →
CLPD REQUIREMENT RELIEF

Exemption / Extension request

Submit your exemption or extension application
Applications are reviewed on a case-by-case basis by the CLPD Affairs Committee. Provide full supporting documentation for your circumstances.
Exemption/Extension Application →
Who may apply for exemption?
•Serious illness preventing attendance during the filing period
•Maternity or paternity leave
•Extended travel outside UAE for professional reasons
•Other exceptional circumstances accepted by the Committee
Who may apply for extension?
•Practitioners who have made significant progress but need additional time
•Exceptional unforeseen circumstances occurring close to the deadline
•New entrants to the roll during the filing period
ℹ️
CLPD requirements may be amended per the procedures set by the Department. All amendments are made on a case-by-case basis through the CLPD Affairs Committee. There is no guarantee that an application will be approved.
For assistance: Training.Development@legal.dubai.gov.ae · +971 4 353 3337

Three paths back on track

Lex analysed your filing history, peer cohort, and 47 available courses · last analysis 14:30 GST · confidence 94%
✦
Lex coach
WORKING ON YOUR PLAN · 47 SIGNALS
v4.2 · GPT-5
Hi Yousef. You're 10 points short with 234 days left, and your peer percentile slipped from 51st to 38th this month — but you're well within recovery range. I've modelled three paths to 16 points by 31 Dec. Your strongest signals: ethics complete (2/2 ✓), demonstrated affinity for arbitration content, and Galadari's mass-booking is open for DIFC Day 28 May. The recommended path costs 5 credits total and clears all 10 points by mid-September with low time burden.
✦ RECOMMENDED · 94% MATCH
The fast lane
2 free e-learning + DIFC Arbitration Day + 1 webinar. Done by 12 Sep.
COST5 credits
HOURS17h
END12 Sep
BUDGET
All-online
5 e-learning modules at your own pace. Hits 50% cap on online mandatory at exactly 4 pts.
COSTfree
HOURS22h
END30 Sep
CAREER LIFT
Specialty deep-dive
Mediation Advocacy + Business Negotiation + 2 e-learning. Adds career value.
COST25 credits
HOURS30h
END20 Oct
⚠
RISK ALERT
96% conf
DIFC Day 28 May closes in 18 days
International Arbitration day at DIFC Gate Village 02 has 3 of 60 seats left and matches your declared specialty. If missed, the next equivalent (Mandatory + Arbitration) is 30 Jun — only 1 attempt before deadline.
VALUE · 2 PTS · MANDATORY
PATTERN INSIGHT
91% conf
You complete e-learning at 2× peer speed
Across 3 completed modules you finished 47% faster than 2018-cohort peers. Two more e-learning modules (4 pts) would take you ~6 hours total — a high-leverage option this weekend.
SUGGESTION · 4 PTS · 6H
CAREER OPPORTUNITY
87% conf
Mediation Advocacy boosts your specialty score
Your firm has 4 partners with mediation accreditation; none below partner level. Adding this credential puts you in the top 8% of your cohort and aligns with arbitration practice growth at the firm.

ACTIVE · ANALYSING 47 SIGNALS
v4.2 · Claude
Good afternoon, Director Hashimi. The 2026 programme is tracking +14.2pp ahead of last year at this point, with 96.1% projected by 31 Dec — your highest forecast in the programme's 11-year history. Three priorities for today: (1) 197 lawyers remain in the critical zone, concentrated in Tax and Immigration practices; (2) 3 accreditation applications have breached SLA — oldest at 18 days; (3) anomaly detected in Al Suwaidi & Co. — 14 lawyers filed identical certificates within 90 seconds, recommend audit.

ANOMALY DETECTION

Pattern irregularities

5 anomalies flagged this week · 2 high priority · Lex confidence threshold ≥85%
⚠
HIGH PRIORITY · CERTIFICATE FRAUD
Al Suwaidi & Co. · 14 identical certificates
14 lawyers from the same firm filed identical certificate hashes within 90 seconds on 8 May. Image hash analysis suggests certificate template was duplicated, not separately issued. Recommend immediate audit and provider verification.
CONFIDENCE 96% DETECTED 2H AGO
HIGH PRIORITY · COMPLIANCE STALL
Tax practice −8.2pp in 30 days
Tax practice area compliance dropped from 81.2% to 73.0% in the past month. Attribution: 14 partner-level lawyers postponed mandatory Q2 sessions. Likely cause: corporate tax law deadline coincided with CLPD deadline. Recommend extension policy review.
CONFIDENCE 92% DETECTED YESTERDAY
⏱
SLA BREACH · ACCREDITATION
3 applications over 10-day SLA
ACC-2026-0128 (KTL Training) at 18 days, ACC-2026-0134 (DIFC Legal) at 12 days, ACC-2026-0141 (Green Law) at 11 days. Average review time has increased 23% this quarter, suggesting reviewer capacity issue.
CONFIDENCE 100% ONGOING
UNUSUAL PATTERN · CANCELLATIONS
DIFC Day cancellations 3.2× normal
DIFC Day on 28 May has received 32 cancellations in the past 7 days vs typical 10. Concentrated in Construction (12) and Banking (9) practices. Possible scheduling conflict with industry event. Investigate and consider rescheduling.
CONFIDENCE 87% DETECTED 4H AGO
INFORMATIONAL · POSITIVE TREND
AI courses 2.4× expected uptake
AI Governance and Responsible Use of AI e-learning enrolment is 2.4× projected. 1,247 lawyers enrolled vs 520 forecast. Drives city-wide compliance lift of +3.1pp. Consider expanding mandatory AI curriculum for 2027.
CONFIDENCE 99% ONGOING
